methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

private.name: Stark Defence

private.name: Vatn Systems

data.narrative:
Greenroom Robotics is a Series A-stage defense and security robotics company founded by Harry Hubbert. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 10 private companies and 2 public comparables operating in the Defense & Security sector, including named privates Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, and Ares Industries, alongside public references DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS.

The peer-median method applies directly to this stage and sector. Three same-stage, same-sector comparables—Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Ares Industries ($150M)—yield a median valuation of $300M and a range spanning $150M to $500M. This peer-median approach represents the primary triangulation anchor given available data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $255M (low) to $345M (high), with a median point of $300M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single quantitative method applied to a three-company peer cohort. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of selected comparables given the heterogeneity typical in frontier defense tech, the absence of revenue or growth metrics to calibrate relative valuation, and limited visibility into Greenroom's competitive differentiation versus named peers. Additional methods such as discounted cash flow or revenue multiple analysis would strengthen conviction but cannot be applied without operational data.
